results.query: What is the full prerequisite chain to reach COMPSCI 162 from scratch?
results.response:
Answer / Plan:
Chain identified: COMPSCI 162 requires COMPSCI 61B and COMPSCI 61C and COMPSCI 70. COMPSCI 61B requires COMPSCI 61A or COMPSCI 88 or ENGIN 7. COMPSCI 61C requires COMPSCI 61A and COMPSCI 61B or COMPSCI 61BL. COMPSCI 61BL requires COMPSCI 61A or COMPSCI 88 or ENGIN 7. [C1, C2, C3, C4]
Next step: Complete the courses in dependency order before attempting COMPSCI 162. [C1, C2, C3, C4]
Why (requirements/prereqs satisfied):
- COMPSCI 162 requires COMPSCI 61B and COMPSCI 61C and COMPSCI 70. [C1, C2, C3, C4]
- COMPSCI 61B requires COMPSCI 61A or COMPSCI 88 or ENGIN 7. [C1, C2, C3, C4]
- COMPSCI 61C requires COMPSCI 61A and COMPSCI 61B or COMPSCI 61BL. [C1, C2, C3, C4]
- COMPSCI 61BL requires COMPSCI 61A or COMPSCI 88 or ENGIN 7. [C1, C2, C3, C4]
Citations:
- [C1] [Source: https://guide.berkeley.edu/courses/compsci/compsci.pdf, COMPSCI 162 Rules & Requirements, accessed 2026-03-27]
- [C2] [Source: https://guide.berkeley.edu/courses/compsci/compsci.pdf, COMPSCI 61B Rules & Requirements, accessed 2026-03-27]

- [C4] [Source: https://guide.berkeley.edu/courses/compsci/compsci.pdf, COMPSCI 61BL Rules & Requirements, accessed 2026-03-27]

results.query: Show all valid paths if a prerequisite allows either COMPSCI 61B or COMPSCI 61BL before an upper-division course.
results.response:
Answer / Plan:
Multiple valid paths: the catalog allows COMPSCI 61C after COMPSCI 61A plus either COMPSCI 61B or COMPSCI 61BL, so one valid route is COMPSCI 61A -> COMPSCI 61B -> COMPSCI 61C -> COMPSCI 161, and another is COMPSCI 61A -> COMPSCI 61BL -> COMPSCI 61C -> COMPSCI 161. [C1, C2]
Next step: Choose the 61B or 61BL branch first, then finish COMPSCI 61C before enrolling in COMPSCI 161. [C1, C2]
Why (requirements/prereqs satisfied):
- COMPSCI 61C accepts either COMPSCI 61B or COMPSCI 61BL after COMPSCI 61A. [C1, C2]
- COMPSCI 161 then requires COMPSCI 61C plus its own listed direct prerequisites. [C1, C2]
Citations:
- [C1] [Source: https://guide.berkeley.edu/courses/compsci/compsci.pdf, COMPSCI 61C Rules & Requirements, accessed 2026-03-27]
- [C2] [Source: https://guide.berkeley.edu/courses/compsci/compsci.pdf, COMPSCI 161 Rules & Requirements, accessed 2026-03-27]

results.query: I am starting fresh. What four-term path gets me to COMPSCI 189 if I can only take two technical courses per term?
results.response:
Answer / Plan:
Recommended plan: a conservative four-term path to COMPSCI 189 is Term 1: COMPSCI 61A and MATH 53; Term 2: COMPSCI 61B and MATH 54; Term 3: COMPSCI 70 and COMPSCI 61C; Term 4: COMPSCI 189 and another eligible upper-division CS course. [C1, C2, C3, C4]
Next step: Verify your math placement or completed calculus background separately if MATH 53 and MATH 54 are still outstanding. [C1, C2, C3, C4]
Why (requirements/prereqs satisfied):
- COMPSCI 189 requires the prerequisite set captured in the Berkeley catalog. [C1, C2, C3, C4]
- This path keeps the technical load at two courses or fewer per term while preserving prerequisite order. [C1, C2, C3, C4]
Citations:
- [C1] [Source: https://guide.berkeley.edu/courses/compsci/compsci.pdf, COMPSCI 61B Rules & Requirements, accessed 2026-03-27]
- [C2] [Source: https://guide.berkeley.edu/courses/compsci/compsci.pdf, COMPSCI 70 Rules & Requirements, accessed 2026-03-27]
- [C3] [Source: https://guide.berkeley.edu/courses/compsci/compsci.pdf, COMPSCI 61C Rules & Requirements, accessed 2026-03-27]

- This sample path assumes you can begin COMPSCI 61A and MATH 53 without additional placement constraints. [C1, C2, C3, C4]

results.query: What is the total number of units required to complete the Berkeley computer science degree?
results.response:
Answer / Plan:
Berkeley's bachelor's degree requires at least 120 units, and the BA in Computer Science also requires the university, campus, college, and major requirements. [C1, C2]
Next step: Use your degree audit to separate major units from remaining campus and college requirements. [C1, C2]
Why (requirements/prereqs satisfied):
- To earn a Berkeley bachelor's degree, students must complete at least 120 semester units and maintain at least a 2.0 cumulative GPA. The senior residence rule requires at least 24 of the remaining units after reaching 90 total units to be completed in residence over at least two semesters, with at least 6 units in each semester. [C1, C2]
- The BA path requires the university and campus requirements plus the College of Computing, Data Science, and Society requirements. The page states that CDSS breadth is the same as the College of Letters and Science Seven-Course Breadth requirement, except that foreign language may be used to satisfy International Studies. The page also notes the bachelor's degree minimum of 120 units and at least 36 upper-division units. [C1, C2]
Citations:
- [C1] [Source: https://undergraduate.catalog.berkeley.edu/undergraduate/degree-requirements/, Units and GPA, accessed 2026-03-27]
- [C2] [Source: https://guide.berkeley.edu/undergraduate/degree-programs/computer-science/, College and Breadth Requirements, accessed 2026-03-27]
Clarifying questions (if needed):
- None
Assumptions / Not in catalog:
- None

results.query: Which parts of the Berkeley computer science degree are core requirements versus electives?
results.response:
Answer / Plan:
For the Berkeley CS BA, the core is the lower-division CS sequence plus the upper-division major-course structure; elective flexibility appears in the five upper-division CS selections, the approved non-CS technical elective, and college breadth choices. [C1, C2]
Next step: Match your completed courses against the lower-division sequence first, then use eligible upper-division courses to fill the remaining major slots. [C1, C2]
Why (requirements/prereqs satisfied):
- The Berkeley BA in Computer Science includes lower-division preparation built around COMPSCI 61A, COMPSCI 61B or COMPSCI 61BL, COMPSCI 70, and COMPSCI 61C, plus approved mathematics support. The plan of study shows five upper-division CS major courses and one approved upper-division non-CS technical elective. [C1, C2]
- The BA major page highlights an upper-division design-course bucket that includes COMPSCI 152, 160, 161, 162, 164, and 168, and the sample schedule labels the upper-division CS major sequence as five major courses. Students also complete one approved upper-division non-CS technical elective. [C1, C2]
Citations:
- [C1] [Source: https://guide.berkeley.edu/undergraduate/degree-programs/computer-science/, Major Requirements, accessed 2026-03-27]
- [C2] [Source: https://guide.berkeley.edu/undergraduate/degree-programs/computer-science/, Upper Division Requirements, accessed 2026-03-27]
Clarifying questions (if needed):
- None
Assumptions / Not in catalog:
- None

results.query: What breadth requirements do computer science students need to satisfy?
results.response:
Answer / Plan:
CS BA students still complete Berkeley college and campus breadth work. The CS BA page says CDSS breadth follows the Letters and Science Seven-Course Breadth structure, with the noted foreign-language exception for International Studies. [C1]
Next step: Check your degree audit or advisor-approved breadth plan before using a breadth course to double-count toward another requirement. [C1]
Why (requirements/prereqs satisfied):
- The BA path requires the university and campus requirements plus the College of Computing, Data Science, and Society requirements. The page states that CDSS breadth is the same as the College of Letters and Science Seven-Course Breadth requirement, except that foreign language may be used to satisfy International Studies. The page also notes the bachelor's degree minimum of 120 units and at least 36 upper-division units. [C1]
Citations:
- [C1] [Source: https://guide.berkeley.edu/undergraduate/degree-programs/computer-science/, College and Breadth Requirements, accessed 2026-03-27]
Clarifying questions (if needed):
- None

results.query: What is the difference between the CS major and the CS minor?
results.response:
Answer / Plan:
The BA major is a full bachelor's program with university, campus, college, and major requirements; the CS minor is a seven-course add-on centered on the lower-division CS sequence plus three upper-division COMPSCI courses. [C1, C2]
Next step: If you are choosing between them, compare your remaining breadth and residence obligations, not just the CS course count. [C1, C2]
Why (requirements/prereqs satisfied):
- UC Berkeley distinguishes between the Bachelor of Arts in Computer Science and the Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences. The BA path is housed in the College of Computing, Data Science, and Society and combines university, campus, college, and major requirements. [C1, C2]
- The Computer Science minor consists of seven courses: COMPSCI 61A, COMPSCI 61B or COMPSCI 61BL, COMPSCI 61C, COMPSCI 70, and three upper-division COMPSCI courses. The page states that at least three upper-division courses for the minor must be taken at Berkeley and that students must earn at least a 2.0 GPA in the upper-division courses used for the minor. [C1, C2]
Citations:
- [C1] [Source: https://guide.berkeley.edu/undergraduate/degree-programs/computer-science/, Choosing a Computer Science Path, accessed 2026-03-27]
- [C2] [Source: https://guide.berkeley.edu/undergraduate/degree-programs/computer-science/, Minor Requirements, accessed 2026-03-27]
Clarifying questions (if needed):
- None
Assumptions / Not in catalog:
- None

results.query: How many units must be completed in residence at the university?
results.response:
Answer / Plan:
Berkeley's senior residence rule requires at least 24 of the remaining units after 90 total units to be completed in residence over at least two semesters, with at least 6 units in each semester. [C1]
Next step: Confirm how your current Berkeley unit total maps to the senior-residence rule before making an accelerated plan. [C1]
Why (requirements/prereqs satisfied):
- To earn a Berkeley bachelor's degree, students must complete at least 120 semester units and maintain at least a 2.0 cumulative GPA. The senior residence rule requires at least 24 of the remaining units after reaching 90 total units to be completed in residence over at least two semesters, with at least 6 units in each semester. [C1]
Citations:
